title: impatiens eryaleia subsp. eryaleia
intro: Impatiens eryaleia subsp. eryaleia is a plant species commonly known as Balsam, which belongs to the Balsam family (Impatiens). The medicinal properties of Impatiens eryaleia subsp. eryaleia include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of balsamic resin. The health benefits of using this plant include the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its use as a topical application for wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Impatiens eryaleia subsp. eryaleia include balsam resin extracts, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to make medicinal products such as cough syrups, salves, and ointments.
